methods.description: The technique used to retrieve the forms described in this study follows the standard strewn-slide technique. This method is used to prepare nannofossil samples from shale for microscopic analysis.

steps.description: Two to four (2 -4) grams of fresh shale was carefully selected from each of the 35 composite ditch cutting samples recovered within a section (8900 -11,000 feet) of an offshore Niger delta oil well.
steps.searchable_summary: select 2-4 grams of shale from ditch cutting samples

steps.description: Oily samples from oil based drilling mud were gently washed with domestic detergent to remove drilling mud.
steps.searchable_summary: wash oily samples with detergent to remove drilling mud

steps.description: Each of the samples was put on a clean white sheet of paper, which folded, and then gently crushed because of the fragile nature of nannofossils using pestle.
steps.searchable_summary: crush shale samples gently using a pestle

steps.description: The crushed materials were then dispersed in distilled water inside a glass vial and stirred with a stirring rod after which it was decanted in another glass tube.
steps.searchable_summary: disperse crushed samples in distilled water and decant

steps.description: Using a disposable pipette, the resulting suspension was pipetted onto a 22 × 40 mm cover slip, then placed on a hot plate and gently dried at a temperature of approximately 50˚C -60˚C.
steps.searchable_summary: pipette suspension onto cover slip and dry on hot plate

steps.description: Thereafter, cover slip was then mounted on a labeled glass slide using two drops of Norland opti-
steps.searchable_summary: mount cover slip on glass slide with Norland opti
